priorities: Zero‑tolerance for reputational risk from labor or supply‑chain scandals
Data‑driven cost‑of‑capital and asymmetric tail‑risk analysis
Alignment of portfolio integrity with his own brand’s clean‑labor image
Liquidity matching for tax liabilities and opportunistic cash deployment
High operational transparency and alpha generation within governance constraints
redLines: Any holding linked to sweatshops, illegal exploitation, wage theft or other labor abuses
Immediate divestment of any consumer/retail company hit by a labor‑exploitation scandal before media exposure
Positions in companies with live media or judicial scrutiny over labor practices, regardless of ESG scores
Supply‑chain governance liabilities in consumer discretionary holdings
Exposure to cheap, unmonitored foreign logistics providers that could trigger regulatory penalties
preferredSectors: Blue‑chip consumer discretionary/retail (with clean supply chains)
Large‑cap international equities across healthcare, technology and energy (subject to governance screening)
Real‑estate assets that meet strict compliance criteria
avoidedSectors: Consumer/discretionary firms with documented labor or supply‑chain violations
Companies reliant on low‑cost, unmonitored foreign logistics
esgFocus: Clean‑labour, local integrity and anti‑boycott risk

investmentBehavior.timeHorizon: Medium to long term (3‑5+ years) with periodic re‑balancing to protect brand reputation
investmentBehavior.liquidity: Requires short‑term high‑quality cash instruments for tax liabilities (2025) and opportunistic art purchases; prefers Swiss cantonal short‑term bonds

lifeEvents.description: Launched a sustainability marketing campaign for his retail brand in Switzerland.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Re‑affirmed willingness to accept lower yields for a clean‑footprint portfolio; tightened ESG screening.

lifeEvents.description: Dividend sweep from private holding company; capital redeployed into the global balanced framework.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Increased cash inflow, reinforced sector‑neutral large‑cap weighting.

lifeEvents.description: Requested detailed fixed‑income duration to match corporate tax liabilities due late 2025.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Adjusted bond ladder to align with tax cash‑flow timing.

lifeEvents.description: Highlighted consumer boycotts from labor scandals as a massive unquantifiable risk.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Strengthened continuous audit of third‑party logistics and manufacturing networks.

lifeEvents.description: Brand expansion into western Switzerland under heavy media and union scrutiny.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Elevated focus on personal transparency and real‑time governance monitoring.

lifeEvents.description: Formal handover to new RM, emphasizing extreme zero‑tolerance for supply‑chain contamination.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Mandate codified in transition documents; compliance filters made non‑negotiable.

lifeEvents.description: Introductory call stating the portfolio must be a "fortress of clean corporate governance".
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Re‑asserted core governance mandate; reinforced need for real‑time scandal alerts.

lifeEvents.description: Critiqued standard ESG scores as useless; demanded immediate flagging of any live labor‑practice controversy.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Shifted ESG monitoring to real‑time media/judicial watchlists, overriding retrospective ratings.

personalProfile.interests: sustainable retail, corporate governance, art collecting
personalProfile.quirks: insists on real‑time scandal monitoring, prefers Swiss cantonal short‑term bonds for idle cash
personalProfile.rapportTriggers: mention of Swiss labour standards, clean supply‑chain initiatives, sustainability projects
personalProfile.avoidTopics: competitor retail brands, political debates, rival sports teams

keyQuotes: My national brand equity is built on clean labor and local integrity. If my name is linked via my portfolio to an international corporate parent caught using sweatshops, illegal exploitation, or wage theft, it creates immediate hypocrisy risk for my own stores.
I will gladly accept a lower Sharpe ratio or lower yields in exchange for a clean portfolio footprint.
If any international consumer or retail brand we hold gets hit with severe labor exploitation allegations, dump the entire position before the local Swiss press can link my name to it.

Standard glossy ESG reporting and backward‑looking sustainability scores are useless to me. ... if there is live media or judicial smoke surrounding their labor practices right now, the position must be flagged immediately before it hits mainstream national press.
My investment portfolio must be a fortress of clean corporate governance. No reputational liabilities, period.
